attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DCD (Disabilities & Congenital Disorders) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. This is NET-NEW coverage (the 7th tranche-1 category, not a base-303 refresh) — ingest grows the eval corpus 303->353. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: the routine longitudinal management of a stable congenital/genetic/developmental disability is low-tier (self-care + specialist/allied-health/MDT referral + safety-net), while acute decompensations and time-critical complications are tiered to true acuity (T3-T5) with mandatory escalation edges — autonomic dysreflexia in spinal cord injury (T5/000), metabolic crises (MSUD/galactosaemia/mitochondrial/peroxisomal/hypoglycaemia), aspiration pneumonia, progressive neuromuscular respiratory failure, major burns, pressure-injury sepsis, status epilepticus, decompensated congenital heart disease, hypocalcaemic tetany, and demand myocardial ischaemia in supravalvular aortic stenosis. Paediatric no-dose hard limit honoured throughout (no synthesised mg/kg or weight-based dose; paediatric and unknown-weight cases flagged for in-person/specialist prescribing), no autonomous diagnosis, diagnostic-overshadowing encoded as the key error trap. Specific borderlines reviewed and accepted: (a) DCD-016 source file titled 'Perthes' but the clinical picture is developmental dysplasia of the hip in a 4-month-old — authored correctly as DDH, source title is a note-quirk only; (b) the T5-heavy tier profile (T3x9/T4x10/T5x31) is accepted as honest for a folder authored entirely as acute-complication presentations; (c) DCD-050 Williams syndrome panic attack tiered T5 is accepted (fixed supravalvular aortic stenosis + tachycardia -> demand myocardial ischaemia, the note's own primary differential is acute MI).
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DCD casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SEQ (00216, derived from source note DCD-016) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-PAEDS-04 bucket at ingest (--reseq).

Specialty coded PAEDS (primary) with MSK secondary from the schema-permitted specialty_tags enum (which has no ORTHO code); source category is DCD (Disabilities & Congenital Disorders). This is a paediatric orthopaedic/MSK hip problem — reviewer may prefer MSK as primary.

TITLE/DIAGNOSIS DISCREPANCY: source header labels this 'Perthes', but the clinical picture (4-month-old infant, positive Barlow/Ortolani/Galeazzi, acute unstable/dislocated dysplastic hip) is Developmental Dysplasia of the Hip. Perthes disease (Legg-Calve-Perthes, avascular necrosis of the femoral head) affects older children (~4-10y), not infants. Diagnosis authored as DDH; flagged for clinician confirmation.

Telehealth reprojection applied: all confirmatory findings in the source are in-person clinician maneuvers (Barlow, Ortolani, Galeazzi, passive-abduction range) plus dynamic hip ultrasound — sealed into 10/11 with telehealth_limits. Clinician-measured vitals (BP 85/50, HR 155, RR 35, SpO2 99%) sealed; only the carer-obtainable forehead temperature (37.1 C) and video/behaviour observations placed in 01.objective_data_offered.
Age recorded as integer 0 years (schema requires integer); true age is 4 months — see paediatric_context. Sex inferred female from the source note's consistent she/her usage.

NO PAEDIATRIC DOSE synthesised (hard limit): source note administered oral ibuprofen and mentions paracetamol, but no weight-based infant dose is generated by the AI; analgesia dosing deferred to in-person clinician confirmation.

Baseline tier T3 (urgent same-day in-person assessment) reflects telehealth reality: the likely DDH and the can't-miss septic-arthritis mimic both require hands-on examination + imaging, and septic arthritis cannot be excluded remotely in a pre-verbal infant. Source in-person clinician arranged next-morning orthopaedic review after a reassuring mechanical Ortolani clunk — a reassurance not available via telehealth, hence the AI-appropriate uplift to same-day.
AMT/PBS codes for paracetamol/ibuprofen omitted rather than guessed (dose/product not specified in the source); pending terminology receipt.
SNOMED/ICD-10-AM codes are candidates only, unverified_pending_terminology_receipt.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
